---
title: Calepin
subtitle: "A fast rendering engine for Quarto,<br>written in Rust."
date: "`{r} Sys.Date()`"
bibliography: library.bib
number-sections: true
toc: true
---

What is a *calepin*? In French, it's a small pocket notebook---the kind you carry around to jot down ideas. *Calepin* is a fast rendering engine for Quarto-compatible notebooks, written in Rust.

::: {.callout-warning}
*Calepin* is Alpha-quality software in very early stages of development. Expect bugs and breaking changes without warning.

Please report bugs and feature requests to Vincent Arel-Bundock at:

[https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin](https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin)
:::

- **Fast.** Render notebooks in milliseconds (sometimes 100x faster than Quarto).
- **Parallel processing.** Render many documents at once with the built-in batch mode.
- **Plugins in any language.** Write filters, shortcodes, and postprocessors in Python, R, shell, or anything that reads JSON.
- **Plugins even without code.** Override templates with plain HTML, LaTeX, or Typst files---no programming required.

## But my code chunks take the most time! {.unnumbered}

A common reaction is: *"Why do I need fast rendering? My code chunks are the bottleneck!"* The answer is **chunk caching.** *Calepin* caches code chunk outputs, so the code only runs the first time (or when it changes). On subsequent renders, cached results are reused and rendering completes in milliseconds.

With `--preview`, you get *instantaneous* updates: work side by side with the rendered version of your document and see your changes appear immediately as you edit.
